скинули в чат статью про RAG + вот мысли:

everyone says RAG is solved now bc vector DB + embeddings cheap. 
we tried at work - latency is insane when you chunk 500 docs and rerank top 50. 
cost actually went UP not down bc we pay for embedding calls + reranker + main LLM every query.

the real issue nobody talks about: retrieval quality plateaus after ~2 weeks when docs change. 
you need continuous re-indexing pipelines, eval sets, human labelers. that's where the money goes.

counter: "just use bigger context windows" - ok but 200k context != finding the right paragraph. 
attention dilution. we A/B tested - full doc in context lost to good retrieval on compliance Q&A.

my bet: RAG vendors selling "drop in vector search" will get replaced by vertical eval infra companies. 
the moat is not pinecone it's whether your retrieval failures are measurable before users notice.

open question - am i wrong that fine-tuning small models on domain beats RAG for stable corp knowledge?

some guy on hn said hybrid always wins. feels like cope when you look at ops burden.
